subject: Inglés
expectation: Comprehend and analyze information from a variety of listening activities to ask and answer questions on social, academic, college, and career topics.

expectation: Listen to and interact with peers during social interactions; read-alouds (fiction and nonfiction texts); oral presentations; and class, group, and partner discussions.

expectation: Use gestures, expressions, and simple words/phrases to demonstrate engagement and understanding in a socially appropriate manner.

expectation: Repeat and respond to chants, poems, songs, and rhymes using phrases, physical movements, etc.

expectation: Contribute to discussions on a variety of social, academic, college, and career topics in diverse contexts and with different audiences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Participate in class, group, and partner discussions by listening attentively, following turn-taking rules, and asking and answering questions using complete sentences and demonstrating confidence about personal experience and texts.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Respond orally to closed- and open-ended questions.

expectation: Evaluate information and determine appropriate responses to answer questions effectively.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Exchange common social and more formal greetings, retell texts, and recount experiences using increasingly detailed complete sentences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use words, phrases, and expanded sentences to express ideas for a variety of purposes (e.g., to communicate needs and desires).

expectation: Expand sentences to provide details (e.g., time, manner, place, cause) in shared language activities guided by the teacher and sometimes independently.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Contribute to social, academic, college, and career conversations using accurate and appropriate language.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Retell conversations and summarize fictional and informational texts. Respond to stories, read-alouds, and presentations orally using a growing number of general, academic, and content-specific words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Provide, justify, and defend opinions or positions in speech.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Offer and support opinions by providing good reasons and increasingly detailed examples from experience and texts.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Share and elaborate on statements, opinions, or arguments using language models or sentence starters (prompts) with increasing independence.

expectation: Adjust language choices according to the task, context, purpose, and audience.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Describe personal experiences using extended vocabulary with a growing number of nouns, noun phrases, adjectives, and verbs to provide details appropriate to the situation.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Plan and deliver different types of oral presentations/reports to express information and support ideas in social, academic, college, and career settings.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Retell texts and recount experiences using complete sentences, key words, and a growing number of general, academic, and domain‐specific words to add detail while speaking.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recite, memorize, or present more complex rhymes, poems, or songs. Foundational Skills The Foundational Skills Standard is aimed at fostering the student’s understanding and working knowledge of print concepts, the alphabetic principle, and other basic conventions of the English writing system. These foundational skills are not independent objectives; rather, they are necessary and important components of an effective, comprehensive reading program. Instruction should be differentiated: good readers will need much less practice with these concepts than struggling readers. The objective is to discern when students need attention in these skills.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Print concepts

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize that the alphabet consists of consonants and vowels.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize that print conveys specific meaning and that pictures may support meaning.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Apply the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Phonological awareness (rhyming, blending, segmentation, deletion, isolation, substitution)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize, match, and produce rhyming words, including nonsense words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Identify syllables through actions (e.g., clapping).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Manipulate sounds and words in shared, guided, and independent activities, such as singing, chanting, or participating in finger plays.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken monosyllabic words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Orally produce monosyllabic words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken monosyllabic words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Segment spoken monosyllabic words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Phonics and word recognition

expectation: Understand and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills to decode short and long sound patterns and their variants both in isolation and in text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled monosyllabic words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Understand spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Decode regularly spelled two three-syllable words with long vowels.

expectation: Decode two- to three-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.

expectation: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.

expectation: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.

expectation: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

expectation: Read (100–150) high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Fluency

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension. (See fluency assessment tools in the appendix.)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read on-level texts with purpose and understanding.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Accurately read on-level texts orally with appropriate rate and expression on successive readings.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary. Reading The Reading Standard places equal importance on the complexity of what students read and the skill with which they read. This standard defines a grade-by-grade level of text complexity that starts with beginning reading and increases up to the college and career-readiness high school level. When reading or viewing multimedia resources, the student must also demonstrate a growing ability to understand or use texts, make connections among ideas and between texts, and use textual evidence to support comprehension.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read critically to make logical inferences and cite specific textual evidence to support conclusions drawn from the text.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With prompting and support, make predictions, describe ideas, phenomena (e.g., erosion), and textual elements (e.g., central message, character traits) using greater detail based on understanding a variety of grade-level and read-aloud texts or multimedia resources.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Distinguish fact vs. opinion.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Distinguish fiction vs. nonfiction.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Identify facts/details that support main idea(s) from the texts.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Determine main ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development. Summarize the key supporting details and ideas.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Analyze how and why individuals, events, or ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings. Analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.

expectation: Analyze the structure of a text, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., sections, chapters, scenes, or stanzas) relate to each other and to the whole.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Describe the major differences between literary and informational texts.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use titles, headings, and illustrations to predict and confirm text topics.

expectation: Use illustrations (picture cues) to identify story details and categorize similarities and differences between characters and details within texts.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Delineate and evaluate an author’s argument through evidence specified in a text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Compare and contrast 2 or more authors’ presentations of similar themes or topics.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read literary texts (e.g., folktale, realistic fiction, animal fantasy, legends, fairytales, poetry) and informational (e.g., science or social science articles) with purpose and understanding. Writing The Writing Standard develops the student’s capacity and skills to plan, revise, edit, and publish many types of writing, such as arguments, informational/explanatory texts, and narratives. This standard stresses the importance of the reading-writing connection by requiring students to draw upon and write about evidence from literary and informational texts/media or when writing for research and investigations.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write arguments to support point of view using valid reasoning and relevant evidence.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write to express feelings, familiar topics, experiences and to describe a picture. Use grade-level appropriate high- frequency words (e.g., sight words list) to write simple sentences (subject + verb + object) in paragraphs of 3–5 sentences. (Include 4 or more words per sentence.)
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write informational texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Work independently and collaborate with peers to draw and write informational texts. Use grade-level appropriate high-frequency words (e.g., sight words list) to write simple sentences (subject + verb + object) in short paragraphs of 3–5 sentences. (Include 4 or more words per sentence.)
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write literary texts to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, details, and structure.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Collaborate with peers to draw and write literary texts. Use grade-level appropriate high-frequency words (e.g., sight words list) to write simple sentences (subject + verb + object) in short paragraphs of 3–5 sentences. (Include 4 or more words per sentence.)
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, and publishing).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, brainstorm ideas for writing on a topic (e.g., additional text, drawings, visual displays, labels) to strengthen writing.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use technology, including the internet, to interact and collaborate with others and produce and publish writing.

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use a multimedia element to enhance a written task. Multimedia elements may include, but are not limited to, drawings, pictures, artifacts, and audio or digital representations. At this grade level, the multimedia element should relate to the task.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Conduct research projects of varying lengths based on focused questions to demonstrate understanding of the subject.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., record science observations or read several books on a single topic to produce a report).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore several how-to books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Gather information from a variety of sources (literary and informational) to draw evidence from grade-level texts to support answers to questions in writing.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write routinely over short and extended periods of time for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recall information to answer a question about a single topic.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, use a combination of drawing, dictating, invented and creative writing, and/or labeling a picture with a few words following a prompt. Language The Language Standard is presented as a summary of the skills and abilities that support the understanding of essential rules of written and spoken English. The vocabulary focuses on understanding and acquiring new general, academic, and content-specific words and phrases.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate command of English grammar and usage conventions.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate command of English grammar and usage conventions when writing or speaking.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use common and proper nouns.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use collective nouns (e.g., group) and possessive nouns.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring regular verbs (e.g., want/wanted, play/played).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use adjectives and adverbs to choose between them depending on what is to be modified.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., in, on, under, in front, behind, next to).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I/me/my, they/them/their, anyone/everything).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use articles (e.g., the, an, etc.) and demonstrative adjectives (e.g., this, that, etc.) appropriately.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Apply appropriate English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling conventions.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate command of English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling conventions when writing.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use punctuation for declarative, imperative, interrogative, and exclamatory sentences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write a letter or letters for short and long vowel sounds (phonemes).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Generalize learned spelling patterns (word families) when writing words (e.g., at: mat/cat/sat, ake: cake/bake/make).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use conventional spelling for basic sight words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words (e.g., said = /s/ /e/ /d/).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use conventional spelling for grade-appropriate words with common spelling patterns and for some irregular words.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Consult reference materials, including dictionaries, to check and correct spelling as needed.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of how language functions in different contexts, making effective choices for meaning, style, and comprehension.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Choose words and phrases for different purposes (e.g., slang, written vs. spoken, formal vs. informal).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With support, apply differences between the conventions of spoken and written English with some degree of accuracy though not perfectly.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting reference materials.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on class reading and content area study, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use illustrations, predictions, and context clues to help identify the meaning of a word or phrase.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Determine the meaning of the new word that is formed when a prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks/looked/looking).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use the root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition/additional)
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use compound words to derive meaning.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and variation in word meanings.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize word relationships and differences in word meanings.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Identify real-life connections between words and their usage (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Distinguish slight differences of meaning between verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Relate word meanings through similar words (synonyms) or opposites (antonyms).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Accurately use a variety of social, academic, and content-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career-readiness level.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading, and being read to. Respond to texts using adjectives and connecting words (e.g., the conjunctions “like” and “because”). Listening/Speaking The Listening/Speaking Standard develops a range of broadly useful oral communication and interpersonal skills, including those used for formal or informal presentations. The student learns to communicate, collaborate, and listen to ideas; strategically integrate information from oral, visual quantitative, and media sources to evaluate what they hear, use, and display, helping to achieve communicative purposes, and adapt speech to context and task.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)
